title: Happy New Year in Afrikaans
description: Title: Afgahani Afgahani Saale Nao Mubbarak Pashto: د نوي کال مبارک وي! (Da nowai kāl mubarak way!) - This is the most common and versatile way to say Happy New Year in Pashto. It literally translates to "May the New Year be blessed!" د نوي کال په مبارکه سره ډک وي! (Da nowai kāl pa mubāraka sare dak wi!) - This wishes the person a New Year filled with blessings. د نو ځ کال به خوښۍ او سلامت تېري (Da no zho kal ba khushali au sehhat teri) - This wishes the person happiness and health in the New Year. Dari: سال نو مبارک! (Sāl-e no mubarak!) - This is the most common and versatile way to say Happy New Year in Dari. It literally translates to "May the New Year be blessed!" سال نو مبارک و پر از خوشی و سلامتی! (Sāl-e no mubarak wa pur az khushali wa sehhati!) - This wishes the person a New Year filled with happiness and health. نوروز بر شما و خانواده تان مبارک! (Nowruz bar shoma wa khānevade tan mubarak!) - This specifically wishes the person a Happy Nowruz, which is the Afghan New Year celebrated on the spring equinox.

title: Happy New Year in Albanian
description: Title: Afrikaans Afrikaans Gelukkige nuwe jaar Gelukkige Nuwejaar! (pronounced: ge-LOK-ki-ge NOO-we-jaar) - This is a more informal way to say Happy New Year, similar to saying "Happy New Year!" in English. Voorspoedige Nuwejaar! (pronounced: voor-SPOE-di-ge NOO-we-jaar) - This simply means "Happy New Year!" and is the most commonly used phrase. Ek wens u en u gesin 'n geseënde Nuwe Jaar vir 2024! (pronounced: ek VENS oo un oo GE-sin un ge-SEEn-de NOO-we-jaar vir twee-duizend-VIER-en-twin-tig) - This means "I wish you and your family a blessed New Year for 2024!" Mag die Nuwe Jaar vir jou gevul wees met vreugde, voorspoed en gesondheid! (pronounced: mag dee NOO-we-jaar vir joo ge-VUL wees met VROO-ge, voor-SPOED en ge-ZOND-heid) - This wishes the person joy, prosperity, and health in the New Year.

title: Happy New Year in Arabic
description: Title: Albanian Albanian Gezuar Vitin e Ri **Gëzuar:**guh-zoo-AR (emphasis on the first syllable) **Vitin:**vee-tin **e Ri:**uh ree Here are some other ways to wish someone a Happy New Year in Albanian, depending on the context: For a close friend or family member: Bëllëfime! Gëzuar Vitin e Ri! (Happy Holidays! Happy New Year!) For a formal situation: Urime të gjitha të mira për Vitin e Ri! (We wish you all the best for the New Year!) To toast with someone: N'shkak! Të na vjenë mirë gjithçka gjatë vitit! (Cheers! May everything go well for us throughout the year!)

title: Happy New Year in Armenian
description: Title: Arabic Arabic Antum salimoun عام جديد سعيد, لتكن لك إجازة عام جديد سعيدة (تقال وقت بداية سنة جديدة) General wishes: كل عام وأنت بخير (Kull 3ām wa-ʾantum bi-khayrin): This is the most common and versatile way to say "Happy New Year" in Arabic. It literally translates to "May every year be good for you." سنة سعيدة (Sana saʿīdah): This simply means "Happy Year" and is a concise and elegant option. عام سعيد (3ām saʿīd): This is the masculine version of "Sana saʿīdah" and can be used for both men and women. More specific wishes: أتمنى لك سنة جديدة مليئة بالصحة والسعادة والنجاح (ʾatamannā laka sanah ǧadīdah maliʾah bi-l-ṣiḥḥah wa-s-saʿādah wa-n-najāḥ): This wishes the person a New Year filled with health, happiness, and success. كل عام وأنت تحقق أهدافك (Kull 3ām wa-ʾantum taḥqiqū ʾahdāfakum): This wishes the person a New Year where they achieve their goals. كل عام وأنت محاط بالأحباء (Kull 3ām wa-ʾantum muḥāṭṭun bi-l-ʾaḥbāb): This wishes the person a New Year surrounded by loved ones. Formal wishes: كل عام وأنتم بخير ومسرورون (Kull 3ām wa-ʾantum bi-khīr wa-masrūrūn): This is a more formal way to say "Happy New Year" and wishes the person both well-being and happiness. تقبل الله أعمالك وجعل عامك الجديد مباركًا (Taqabbal Allāhu ʾaʿmālak wa-jaʿala ʿāmak al-jadīd mubārakan): This is a religious wish that asks God to accept the person's deeds and bless their New Year.
